title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: labno2 R package extracts DNA sample identifiers (lab numbers, worksheet numbers, suffixes) filenames used North West Genomic Laboratory Hub. Lab numbers 8-digit strings first 2 digits encode year (e.g., 26000001 = first sample 2026). Important: patient-identifiable information ever committed. Test data uses fictional names Tolstoy novels (Pierre Bezukhov, Anna Karenina, Ivan Ilyich) generic identifiers (WS123456, 12345678).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Run tests: Run single test file: Build documentation (roxygen2): Check package: Install locally:

title: CLAUDE.md
text: package thin layered structure: R/regex-ids.R — single source truth regex patterns. regex_ids() returns named list worksheet labno_suffix patterns. extraction functions call ; change patterns . R/extract-*.R — three single-purpose extractors (extract_worksheet, extract_labno, extract_suffix) call regex_ids() use stringr::str_extract capture groups. labno_suffix regex uses non-digit boundary guards (\D+) avoid matching 8 digits inside longer numeric string. R/mutate-ids.R — mutate_ids(df, id_col = filename) applies three extractors row-wise via dplyr::rowwise() adds derived columns (labno_suffix, labno_suffix_worksheet). Uses tidy evaluation id_col. R/read_csv_ids.R — convenience wrapper: reads CSV readr::read_csv, adds filepath filename columns, calls mutate_ids().

title: CLAUDE.md
text: labno_suffix pattern (^|\D+)(\d{8})(|b|c|d|)(\D+|$). Group 2 labno, group 3 suffix. non-digit guards sides prevent extracting 8 digits longer numbers (e.g., 10-digit string). Samples tested multiple times worksheet get letter suffix (, b, c, d); suffix empty string (NA) absent.

title: Exploratory data analysis with labtools
text: multiple files genetic results , sample identifiers filenames. file consists results different genes. purpose example, randomly generated “score” gene. real clinical data. want get results one dataframe, along relevant identifiers.
code: files <- list.files(path = "data/",            pattern = ".*.csv",            full.names = TRUE)  files #> [1] "data//WS123456_12345678a_PierreBEZUKHOV.csv" #> [2] "data//WS123456_12345678b_PierreBEZUKHOV.csv" #> [3] "data//WS123456_12345678c_PierreBEZUKHOV.csv" #> [4] "data//WS123456_23456789_AnnaKARENINA.csv"    #> [5] "data//WS123456_34567890_IvanILYCH.csv" #> # A tibble: 10 × 2 #>    gene   score #>    <chr>  <dbl> #>  1 gene1  97.7  #>  2 gene2  11.3  #>  3 gene3  98.9  #>  4 gene4  64.0  #>  5 gene5   3.49 #>  6 gene6  11.4  #>  7 gene7  86.8  #>  8 gene8  13.8  #>  9 gene9  49.7  #> 10 gene10 10.6

title: Exploratory data analysis with labtools
text: can single file using read_csv_with_filename function labtools. Using map purrr can iterate function file list bind data together. gives data single dataframe row annotated correct sample identifiers. can explore collated data using ggplot2.
code: read_csv_with_filename(files[1]) #> # A tibble: 10 × 4 #>    gene   score filepath                                    filename             #>    <chr>  <dbl> <chr>                                       <chr>                #>  1 gene1  97.7  data//WS123456_12345678a_PierreBEZUKHOV.csv WS123456_12345678a_… #>  2 gene2  11.3  data//WS123456_12345678a_PierreBEZUKHOV.csv WS123456_12345678a_… #>  3 gene3  98.9  data//WS123456_12345678a_PierreBEZUKHOV.csv WS123456_12345678a_… #>  4 gene4  64.0  data//WS123456_12345678a_PierreBEZUKHOV.csv WS123456_12345678a_… #>  5 gene5   3.49 data//WS123456_12345678a_PierreBEZUKHOV.csv WS123456_12345678a_… #>  6 gene6  11.4  data//WS123456_12345678a_PierreBEZUKHOV.csv WS123456_12345678a_… #>  7 gene7  86.8  data//WS123456_12345678a_PierreBEZUKHOV.csv WS123456_12345678a_… #>  8 gene8  13.8  data//WS123456_12345678a_PierreBEZUKHOV.csv WS123456_12345678a_… #>  9 gene9  49.7  data//WS123456_12345678a_PierreBEZUKHOV.csv WS123456_12345678a_… #> 10 gene10 10.6  data//WS123456_12345678a_PierreBEZUKHOV.csv WS123456_12345678a_… all_data <- files |>    map(\(files) read_csv_with_filename(files)) |>    list_rbind() |>    mutate_ids()

title: Exploratory data analysis with labtools
text: labtools designed help exploratory data analysis multiple files quickly reproducibly. built around tidyverse packages including stringr, readr dplyr. labtools aims include functionality shared across multiple projects . shown example: common problem collating data files include identifiers filenames consistent format worksheet, lab number suffix.

title: Exploratory data analysis with labtools
text: currently function extracting patient names labtools following reasons: Patient names always included filenames. data analysed cloud, patient names removed filenames, means can files mixture patient names patient names depending processed. samples people. samples control samples, mixtures different DNA samples cell lines. samples may given “name” may , name may may follow standard format “forename surname”. Example: “HorizonPositiveControl”. samples may research. Patient samples collected research anonymised, research identifier may included place sample name. research identifier may also include numbers non-letter characters. Example: “study0001” reasons mean designing regular expression identify patient name filename complicated. One option use position name string relative consistent identifiers, like lab number worksheet number, inflexible order identifiers changes.
